resources.string: Set the duration for which the sequence remains active during runtime.
Name
Set the type for ending the active sequence.
Time
Specify the event name or message used for broadcasting.
Set the method for event communication.
Set this location as the initial spawn point.
Spawn the player at a random position within a radius centered around the device.
Set the spawn type.
Spawns neutral players.
Assigns players to a team (color group) and spawns them.
Select the player data. This data can be added in the game settings under the player section.
Set how the spawning character appears. • Balloon: Drops with a balloon. Cannot be canceled manually. • Balloon Glide: Glides with a balloon. Can be canceled manually.
Set the camera transition type when it ends. • Return To Player: Switch to the player camera. • Return To Another Camera: Switch to a designated camera.
Set the camera state after the sequence ends. • Keep State: Restore the initial camera settings. • Recover State: Maintain the final position after the sequence ends.

Set the condition for currently activated camera. • Infinity: Keep the camera active indefinitely until an event ends it. • By Camera Path: End the camera when it reaches the path's end. • Time Value: End the camera after the specified duration.
Set the camera state. • Fixed Cam: Fixed-position camera. • Dolly Cam: Moving camera.
Set the target the camera looks at.
Set the camera's viewing direction while moving. • Checked: Keep the camera direction fixed. • Unchecked: Adjust the camera direction according to the path.
Set the camera's field of view (FOV).
Set the minimum render distance for the camera.
Set the maximum render distance for the camera.
Set the default movement speed when using the camera path.
Set the camera roll angle along the path.
Set individual movement speeds between path points.
Rotate the camera up or down relative to the horizontal axis. • + Value: Rotate downward. • - Value: Rotate upward.
Rotate the camera left or right relative to the vertical axis. • + Value: Rotate left. • - Value: Rotate right.
Set the delay time before the camera moves after reaching a path point.
Display the length of the path.
Display the current camera position.
Set the item name.
Assign a button slot for item usage.
Set the item usage method. • START: Trigger animation when pressing the button. • END: Trigger animation when releasing the button.
Set the maximum number of items a player can hold.
Set the cooldown time before the item can be reused.
Set the icon displayed on the HUD button.
Select the part where the item is placed. • None: Do not place the item. • Head_Acc: Head • Face_Acc: Face • Back_Acc: Back • Right_Weapon: Right hand • Left_Weapon: Left hand.
Enable item stacking. • Stack Time: Time required to charge the item. • Max Stack: Maximum stackable amount.
Set the item's position offset when attached to a part.
Set the item's rotation offset when attached to a part.
Set the item's scale offset when attached to a part.
Change the default animation when the item is equipped.
Change the running animation when the item is equipped.
Change the attack animation when the item is equipped.
Apply the animation to the upper body only. • Checked: Apply animation to the entire body. • Unchecked: Apply animation only to the upper body.
Set the minimum number of players required to start the game.
Set the maximum number of players allowed in the game.
Set the player recruitment condition. • Forbidden: Start the game when the minimum number of players is reached. • Allowed: Continue recruiting until the maximum number of players is reached.
Set the waiting time for players to join until the maximum count is reached.
Set the duration to stay on the Pre-Game Scene before the game starts, regardless of player recruitment status.
Define a custom Lua script for the entire game.
Set the character's maximum movement speed.
Set the character's rotation speed.
Set variables to apply to the player or character.
Define a custom Lua script for the character.
Set the initial life count at game start.
Set the maximum number of lives a player can hold.
Display the player's life count on the HUD.
Display the player's nameplate on the HUD.
Set the initial number of Stars the player starts with.
Set the maximum number of Stars a player can hold.
Assign the scene file used for this stage.
Set the stage icon displayed in the game UI.
Set the number of stars required for the exchange.
Set the item acquired by exchanging stars.
Set the number of items received per exchange.
Using the same Value key will not work properly.
Please check the Variable component and Value key setup on the linked object.
